title: DTS Registry
description: JSON-LD schema for the DTS Registry, a DCAT Catalog of API instances that implement the Distributed Text Services (DTS) specification.

@context.description: JSON-LD context. MUST include the DTS v1.0 context as the first entry, followed by the registry-specific inline context that introduces dcat: terms and locally-defined terms.

@type.description: Top-level type. MUST be "Catalog" (resolves to dcat:Catalog).

title.description: Human-readable name of the registry (resolves to dts:title).

description.description: Human-readable description of the registry (resolves to dts:description).

entries.description: List of registered DTS API instances (resolves to dcat:service).

Entry.description: A single DTS API instance, modelled as a dcat:DataService whose @id is the URL of its DTS Entry Endpoint.

@id.description: Stable identifier for the API instance. By convention, the URL of its DTS Entry Endpoint.

@type.description: MUST be "DataService" (resolves to dcat:DataService). The entry's @id is the URL of the DTS Entry Endpoint; dereferencing that URL yields a dts:EntryPoint document with its own @context, so the registry does not re-assert that type here.

title.description: Human-readable name of the API instance (resolves to dts:title).

description.description: Free-text description of the API instance and its corpus (resolves to dts:description).

dtsVersion.description: Version of the DTS specification implemented by this instance (resolves to dts:dtsVersion). MUST be one of the published spec literals, "1-draft" for implementations the upstream Known Corpora list labels as a 1.0 draft, "pre-alpha" for implementations known to predate the draft series entirely, or "unknown" when the endpoint serves a valid response but does not advertise a dtsVersion field.
dtsVersion.$comment:
1.0       = the published v1.0 specification (https://dtsapi.org/versions/v1.0).
1.0rc1    = the 1.0 release candidate (https://distributed-text-services.github.io/specifications/versions/1.0rc1/).
1-alpha   = the 1-alpha specification (https://distributed-text-services.github.io/specifications/versions/1-alpha/). The unstable branch also self-reports as 1-alpha.
1-draft   = a registry-local umbrella tag for implementations the upstream Known Corpora list labels as "1.0 Draft" (https://dtsapi.org/specifications/#known-corpora-accessible-via-the-dts-api). The only published draft directory in the specifications repository is 1-draft2; the dtsVersion field itself was introduced at 1-alpha, so 1-draft-era responses do not advertise a version. Use 1-draft when prose evidence labels an instance as a 1.0 draft without pinning it to a specific draft number; use 1-draft2 (and add it to this enum) when there is positive evidence an instance targets that specific draft.
pre-alpha = a registry-local marker reserved for implementations with positive evidence of predating the draft series entirely.
unknown   = the endpoint serves a valid DTS response but does not include a dtsVersion field, and we have no external evidence pinning it to a specific spec version.

1.0, 1.0rc1, and 1-alpha are exact literals taken from the DTS specification. 1-draft, pre-alpha, and unknown are registry-local conventions and never appear in DTS responses. The canonical list of published versions and their dtsVersion strings is maintained in the specifications repository:
  - https://dtsapi.org/  (links to every published version)
  - https://github.com/distributed-text-services/specifications/tree/main/versions  (one directory per version; each version's README contains JSON examples whose dtsVersion field is the canonical literal)

status.description: Operational status of the API instance (locally-defined term).
status.$comment:
production    = a stable, actively maintained service intended for real-world use.
development   = a service intended for production but currently incomplete or pre-release. Distinct from experimental (which is research-oriented) in that the maintainers' goal is a production-grade service.
demo          = a publicly accessible demonstration; data and availability may be unstable.
experimental  = a research-oriented instance with no production-grade aspirations; uptime and conformance are not guaranteed.
deprecated    = a previously listed instance that is no longer maintained or recommended.

endpointURL.description: Runtime URL of the DTS Entry Endpoint (resolves to dcat:endpointURL). Typically equal to @id, but kept distinct so that a service that moves can preserve its logical identifier.

contactPoint.description: Contact persons or organizations for this API instance (resolves to dcat:contactPoint).

ContactPoint.description: A contact for a DTS API instance, modelled as a vCard Kind (vcard:Individual or vcard:Organization).

@type.description: MUST be "Individual" (vcard:Individual) for a person, or "Organization" (vcard:Organization) for an institution.

fullName.description: Formatted name of the contact (resolves to vcard:fn).

email.description: Email address as a mailto: IRI (resolves to vcard:hasEmail).
